title: High Regards
content:
How much more prolific,
 of a writer,
 I could become?

If only advanced by age?

I searched longingly
 for some poetry to indulge in,
and what I found
 wasn't satisfactory.

These poets,
 albeit literate and eloquent,

 lacked abstraction.

Lacking the romance that writing brings.
Rooted solely in events,
 poetry inspired by experience,

rather than for expression of misunderstanding.

And I found this to be disappointing.
Such writers are heralded as great,
 who acclaimed?
Perhaps I am lacking in the richness of my perception,
but these writers of acclaim fall short;

Of my artistic glory.

title: Ebb Tide
content:
In my youth,
 I thought it wise to mark my name
 on the face of the Earth;

 In my adolescence
 I thought it better to mark my name
 in the stars;

 In my present
 I find it best to mark my name
 in those hearts.

 The Earth will be consumed,
 the stars will become dim.
 The hearts of those will not let go,

 until the brink sets in.

 Perhaps it is narcissistic
 to wish for such grandiose of a legacy…
 But man inherently strives for such.
 I am no exception.

 I have stared longingly into space,
 just as I have my lover’s;
 Longing all the same…

 to be remembered.

 For who am I without my memories?
 My trials and struggles;
 who am I without a thought to my being?
 Though I recognize I am more than that;

 my soul seeks something more than that.

 “Like a tide at its ebb,
 I’m at peace in the web of your arms.”
 But I never am,

 and I never will be.

title: Dear John Merrick
content:
I see those luxurious amenities;
 And architecture so grand;
 understanding it is not meant for me.

 And my words be set in stone;
 ever so in my poetry.

 So how then can I condemn myself?

 For it I know all too well in my ego now,
 that my vanity is certain.

 I have seen your loving heart.
 I have observed your gentle soul.
 How can man possibly be so abhorred?

 To wish so sorrowfully for such things,
 is to neglect that which I have;
 and that which you,
 were ultimately thankful for.

 You are no monster.
 But I?

 I am a disgusting excuse for a man.

title: Disgust
content:
Your temptation is disgusting;
 and it disgusts me.

 I am a modern day philosopher
 through and through.
 And I find my stance on this societal norm
 to be quite reminiscent
 of those who held it previously.

 I find my values to be quite synonymous
 with those ancient founders of present thought.
 Those men were praised in their time,
 and even acclaimed today.

 But for I to conclude the same as them;

 scorn.

 To be labeled a labeler;
 oh the hypocrisy…

 Men of acclaim today;
 sit on their mighty throne.
 Enshrined by politicians,
 enamored by the masses,
 lifted high in praise
 due to their success
 in having such a remarkable mind.

 Though I suppose it is just as evil to envy,
 than it is to gloat
 in such a position.

 A truly wise man concerns himself not.

 However;
 I stare in discontentment
 as I contemplate my life.

 Ninety nine times ninety men
 doing quite the opposite.
 I am a genius with respect to the masses.
 I am an idiot with respect to logic.
 For in my mind
 this mentality is not useful.
 To them who deem themselves wise,
 it is the only thing
 that holds but any value.

 Because they lack true abstraction.

title: Escape
content:
Everything feels useless.
 I feel empty.
 And hot-headed.
 And directionless.
 And I am scared.
 I feel purposeless.

 In this music,
 in this life.
 It’s all too complex.

 And my mind is filled with patterns.
 And my soul is filled with sounds.
 And spring is beautiful.
 But I want more than here.
 I want a fantastical world to escape to.
 But I suppose
 I can’t avoid this place forever;
 this beautiful sun.
 These familiar smells.

 Am I doomed?
 Does this graphite make it so?
 Do these songs fill me with hope?
 Or are they another escape?

 Will anyone ever read my writing?

 I am certain they will.

title: Romanticism
content:
I am a man
 with dreams too big
 for his own heart.

 I am a god
 who cannot even control himself.

 I hate it all;
 yet I can hold a conversation.

 I am bound and amazed
 by my own mind.
 I chew and bite my way
 out of this body.

title: Cruelty Dissonance
content:
We rode out onto the water,
 and I took in the breeze;
 these March days;
 coming April showers
 remind me of not so long ago
 when I rose up on that hill

 and saw her.

 And my heart was set ablaze,
 and the music filled my ears…
 For in that moment
 I saw true beauty
 undisputed;
 unrequited.

 She was so pure;
 and so precious;
 uncorrupted by sin.

 The pain was never intentional.

 How is it
 that I can write so lovingly
 about someone who hurt me?
 Surely this must mean that love;
 is more powerful
 than pain.

 Truly I tell you,
 living has been a joy.
 Through every tide;
 through every calm.

title: Shoulder
Hesitancy
content:
I feel as if my very existence
 has lost its meaning.
 And my timely soul
 is out of its home.

 So many books left behind.
 Just like centuries old.

 My heart torn in three,
 my love lost its life.

 Wherefore I go?

 I know the truth makes me ugly.
 and destitute.
 And it scares me
 when it all feels too real;
 it all feels too fake.

 God I asked for signs
 and they leave me the very next day.

 I feel the tears well up
 and I get I cannot cry.

 Words words words.

title: Suspense
content:
I am a bit fearful.
 At the prospects of life…
 All this talk of success,
 how am I to succeed?
 With what attributes,
 do I determine the future
 I have been set with?

 I am a flightless bird,
 at the entrance of heaven.

 I am a powerless ram,
 at the stairway to hell.

 Imagine sitting on the rings of Saturn…

title: Contrary
content:
As I return to the valley
 of the shadow of death,
 I longingly wish for simpler days.

 My curls hang low
 on my shoulders.
 Wishing,
 waiting for a miracle.

 As the whispers hit my ears;
 memories collide in a simple,
 unyielding way.

 Abstraction ruling over everything.

 I am inevitably
 a vagabond;
 corpsed to a life of romance;
 yielding love.
 And poems yielding faith.

 I turned to look beside me;
 and I see them in the distance,
 like pillars of my past.
 Cracking in the face of my present.
 Collapsing in the advent
 of my future.

title: Veiled
content:
I will let the ravens
 pick apart my throne;
 the tree-tops
 are threatening me from a distance.

 My poems are still riddled
 with discrepancy.
 But there is a face in my dreams,
 they yell;
 they scream.
 But I don’t hear a thing.

 The skies fill with artificial smoke,
 as they block the sun.
 A poison in the air.
 Collapsing my lungs.

 The face gets closer to me.
 The look of myself.

 The unwavering sun.
 An evil reminds me in the day.
 Cast aside your disbelief.
 Look up unto the stars.
 And become helpless.
 And the monster
 whispers to me a sentence.

 Which I cannot refuse to acknowledge.

 They are right.
 It existed;
 despite its disappearance.
 I will let the ravens
 peck every bone.
 If it means the annihilation
 of my retrospection.

 The wind is my enemy;

 only when I write.

title: Paranoid
content:
I am hurt.
 Torn by the past
 of what was;
 irreversible to a less hopeful picture in front of me.

 Though my love may adapt;
 that beauty
 will never return to me.

 I am scared;
 tortured by the thoughts
 that plague my existing life.
 Never a corner that turns
 which I am not paranoid…

 I am lost.
 For my love,
 for my peace,
 had since left me.
 And left me
 with no choice but nihilism.

 The world is dark;
 my letters so far as to repetition.

title: Hurt
content:
I stare into the eyes
 of the one who resents me,
 and I do not look away.

 Pain has filled the beating heart
 of those I have dismayed.

 Blinded by a madness
 that bled into my water;

 I break apart the only people
 who I called the others.

 Tune in hard,
 I play a song.
 A melody complete.
 Forevermore;
 I tip over
 and let the blood cover my feet.

 Listen close
 to him who hears;
 and has the eyes to see:

 A spinal column decompressed
 by his society
 mix and matched
 I told a tale as bright and tall
 as a star.

 Never once;
 did I ever think
 I’d be the epitome.
 I cannot remove you
 from my screen,
 your eyes
 they bleed right through.

 At last I take my final breath.
 Looking at
 your darkened hue.

title: Vanity
content:
Never to be found
 is how I intend to live;
 but fame in any respect
 sounds amazing.

 Critical of every flaw;
 and imperfection.
 Up until this day,
 not for them,

 still not for me.

 And it remains the only success.
 Behind these almond eyes

 is something far more profound.

title: Jagged Crooked
content:
Every waking moment;
 I labored towards destruction.
 I close my eyes
 and revel in the disastrousness of reality.

 Shield my eyes from this pain;
 from this carnival.

 The piano ringing;
 the light of my life beginning.

 Dine in my fantasy.
 Feast upon every aspect of me.
 Feast,
 feast;
 devour.
 Until I am no more.

 Kill me;
 so slowly;
 that I fall in love
 with the idea of death.

 Play for yourself a song;
 composed by yours truly;
 and watch my anger quell.
 Watch the raw emotion boil.

 And I’ll write
 and write
 and write
 and rip apart every aspect
 of myself.
 My anger giving me insight
 into who I am;

 because they were.

 I am that canvas.
 Reflective of every embodiment.
 And I find that it is sharp,
 and blocky
 and crooked like my soul.

 Blaming those that control me
 for all my insecurities.
 Finding that I am too guilty
 for all my crooked ways.

 All my complaints
 contributing to nothing;
 and crescendoing when I create.

 I pick apart everything;
 in a vulnerable display of love for myself.
 Only to die to these words tomorrow.

 How beautiful could I be,
 if only I were special?

title: Condemnation
content:
In the desperation towards greatness;
 I have fallen to envy.
 This envy
 has led me down a path of sin.

 In my attempts for discipline,
 I am reminded of this evil.
 Hoping not only that
 I can be redeemed
 for my lack of control,
 but that in my strive for more;

 my lack can catch up.

 In my pursuit of discipline,
 I have encountered many devils,
 much like myself.
 These devils
 plague my mind with lethargy.
 For the greatest damnation to an artist;

 is to riddle them with sloth.

 The greatest weakness to a creative;
 is to take away his mind.
 I am an artist without a single medium.
 And I somehow make time to write.

 Read these words carefully;
 my writing is inspired by song.
 Thus once I find the perfect song;

 I can write something to end it all.

title: Dimentia
content:
My mind is beginning to decay.
 With every mention of my memories
 I lack it more and more.

 Who will take my journals;
 is there a man that is worthy?
 Who will carry on,
 that I can remember?

title: Existentialism Short
content:
To me,
 there is no comfort in the infinite.
 Only an end to fear.
 Perhaps this is what they fear…
 Those who seek to dim our illuminance.

 I suppose that after eons;
 I’ll be ready to ascend.
 But for now;
 in my finite mind,
 no philosophy can suffice;

 no excuse can soothe me.

title: Face on my journal
content:
Lay down for the 6,150th time.
 Dream just the same,
 let all your earthly worries
 carry you away.
 Into a world
 of your own design…

 Do you miss the skies?
 I grieve that I cannot grieve.
 I laugh at the pain
 that ails me.
 And when silence whispers;
 I hear not its wisdom;

 for my ears are not open,
 and my eyes cannot see.

 I blame no one but myself.
 My sight was once open;
 my ears once heard.

 Laboring over disastrous,
 instead of a salvation
 once redeemed.

 Why do I toil to perfect
 something that is beautiful,
 imperfect?

 I spent every second of my past
 envisioning my future.

title: Mindfulness
content:
One day our reservoir will dry up,
 and theirs will overflow.
 One day the light we hold so dear,
 will no longer illuminate the darkness.

 Ash will weigh heavier
 than the fires that created it.

 This is the future of our world.

title: Envy
content:
“Oh my heart aches so frivolously.”

 I cannot bear the pain in my lungs.
 From head to toe,
 my body cries,
 but I cannot mend it.
 I cannot heal it.
 For this insatiable taste
 can only be satisfied by one;
 by another.

 I just want to recall
 a recollection of times,
 living life so blissfully,
 not a single care in mind.

 But now the troubles of the age
 have caught up to me.
 So cold and bittersweet.

 I envy all the other boys.
 For them,
 I cannot compete.
 The sadness of the century
 leaves me looking quite the fool,
 when compared to all the problems
 the army faces on their own.

 I just want someone to hold me.
 So maybe I can hold them back.
 The jealousy fights heavily
 in this poor old soul.
 I'm blessed from inside out,
 but with no one to share my world.

 God, I really hate this feeling,
 and I don't use that word a lot.
 But seeing them so happy
 scares me until I'm not.

 There's a million cataclysmic things
 that could end the world tonight,
 so I'd rather spend my evenings

 with someone by my side.

title: Grandmas Sky
content:
A siren sounds,
 a window blows,
 the final minutes,
 despots fall.
 Everyone stands,
 awaiting all beyond what seems so far.
 What felt so long…
 Destined were you?
 Doomed are you?
 Can it fit?
 The words?
 Can you describe the scene?
 Is it difficult?
 In my mind,
 I see it clearly.

 I see it beautifully,

 the past and present
 all at once,
 all together.

 Keep on bawling.
 It will solve nothing.
 The galaxies are meant for exploring.

 A crimson chromosome,
 throughout your body.
 Memories lost forever?
 Could any single man
 create something so vast?
 So clever?

 Lavish and loving,
 disruptive corrupting.
 I'll wait forever;
 tied together.
 Let us dance.
 Cosmic tremors;
 crafted carefully,
 woven pieces,
 sewn like feathers,
 among the void;

 It is now or never.

 He made a promise.
 He'd watch them all.
 But soon they'd fade like everything else.

 Obelisks watch from the shoreline.
 They're scattered all over the earth.
 The architects saw
 that they were pleasing,
 as they had spent a millennium
 in an attempt to perfect.
 And the edifices still remain a mystery.
 Without a single moment to spare,
 men set out on a journey,

 a voyage. (This poem had no date)
